steps: Go to https://portal.azure.com and sign in with your Microsoft account
Navigate to 'Azure Active Directory' (or 'Microsoft Entra ID') > 'App registrations'
Enter a name for your application
Under 'Supported account types', select 'Accounts in any organizational directory and personal Microsoft accounts' to support OneDrive personal and business
Under 'Redirect URI', select 'Web' and enter your redirect URI (e.g. http://localhost:3000/callback for local dev)
On the app overview page, copy the 'Application (client) ID' — this is your Client ID
In the left sidebar, click 'Certificates & secrets', then click '+ New client secret'
Enter a description and choose an expiration, then click 'Add'
Copy the client secret value immediately — it will not be shown again
In the left sidebar, click 'API permissions', then 'Add a permission' > 'Microsoft Graph' > 'Delegated permissions'
Add Files.ReadWrite and Files.Read.All permissions, then click 'Add permissions'

credential_fields.name: client_id
credential_fields.label: Application (Client) ID
credential_fields.description: Your Azure app registration Application (Client) ID

credential_fields.name: client_secret
credential_fields.label: Client Secret
credential_fields.description: Your Azure app registration client secret value — shown only at creation

notes: Free Microsoft accounts include 5 GB OneDrive storage; Microsoft 365 subscribers get 1 TB
Client secret is shown only once at creation — copy it immediately
Client secrets expire (max 2 years); rotate before expiration to avoid service interruption
Required scope for full access: Files.ReadWrite or Files.ReadWrite.All
For personal OneDrive, use tenant 'common'; for Azure AD accounts use your specific tenant ID

test_endpoint.description: Returns the current user's default OneDrive drive
